name: Hybrid Git Sync

releases.author: walkskyer
releases.description:
# Hybrid Git Sync v1.0.5

## What's Changed

### Bug Fixes
- Replace `setDestructive()` with CSS class for backward compatibility
- Maintain `minAppVersion` at 0.15.0 to support older Obsidian versions

### Improvements
- Update README documentation (English and Chinese)
- Add OAuth authentication section to docs
- Add quick start guide for GitHub/Git/API modes

## Installation

1. Download `hybrid-git-sync-1.0.5.zip`
2. Extract to `.obsidian/plugins/hybrid-git-sync/`
3. Enable the plugin in Obsidian settings

## Quick Start

1. Open plugin settings
2. Click **Connect** to authorize with GitHub
3. Select a repository from the dropdown
4. Start syncing!

## Compatibility

- Minimum Obsidian version: 0.15.0
- Supported platforms: Windows, macOS, Linux, Android, iOS

## Changelog

- v1.0.5 — Backward compatibility fix, documentation updates
- v1.0.4 — Code quality fixes, Obsidian review compliance
- v1.0.3 — GitHub OAuth support, UX improvements
- v1.0.2 — Error handling improvements
- v1.0.1 — Auto-detect backend, i18n improvements
- v1.0.0 — Initial release

# Hybrid Git Sync v1.0.4

## What's Changed

### Bug Fixes
- Fix inline styles in OAuth modal (use CSS classes instead)
- Fix Promise handling in command callbacks
- Replace deprecated `setWarning` with `setDestructive`
- Remove unused imports
- Extract async command logic to separate methods

### Code Quality
- Zero `any` types in source code
- All Obsidian plugin review warnings addressed
- Proper TypeScript types throughout

## Installation

1. Download `hybrid-git-sync-1.0.4.zip`
2. Extract to `.obsidian/plugins/hybrid-git-sync/`
3. Enable the plugin in Obsidian settings

## Quick Start

1. Open plugin settings
2. Click **Connect** to authorize with GitHub
3. Select a repository from the dropdown
4. Start syncing!

## Changelog

- v1.0.4 — Code quality fixes, Obsidian review compliance
- v1.0.3 — GitHub OAuth support, UX improvements
- v1.0.2 — Error handling improvements
- v1.0.1 — Auto-detect backend, i18n improvements
- v1.0.0 — Initial release

releases.author: walkskyer
releases.description:
# Hybrid Git Sync v1.0.3

## What's Changed

### New Features
- **GitHub OAuth Device Flow** — Connect your GitHub account with one click
  - No need to manually generate Personal Access Tokens
  - Auto-list your repositories after authorization
  - Select a repository from dropdown to auto-configure sync
- **OAuth Modal** — Persistent authorization dialog with:
  - Large code display for easy copying
  - Copy code button
  - Open browser button
  - Countdown timer

### Bug Fixes
- Fix OAuth polling using wrong code (`user_code` vs `device_code`)
- Fix OAuth code not being selectable/copyable
- Fix OAuth notice disappearing too quickly (replaced with modal)
- Fix `any` type in `loadRepos` method

### Improvements
- Better OAuth UX with modal instead of Notice
- Code quality improvements (zero `any` types)

## Installation

1. Download `hybrid-git-sync-1.0.3.zip`
2. Extract to `.obsidian/plugins/hybrid-git-sync/`
3. Enable the plugin in Obsidian settings

## Quick Start (GitHub)

1. Open plugin settings
2. Click **Connect** to authorize with GitHub
3. Select a repository from the dropdown
4. Start syncing!

## Changelog

- v1.0.3 — GitHub OAuth support, UX improvements
- v1.0.2 — Error handling improvements, code quality fixes
- v1.0.1 — Auto-detect backend, i18n improvements
- v1.0.0 — Initial release

releases.author: walkskyer
releases.description:
# Hybrid Git Sync v1.0.2

## What's Changed

### Improvements
- **Better error handling** — Add `getErrorMessage()` and `toError()` helper functions for consistent error handling across all modules
- **Code quality fixes** — Address all Obsidian plugin review warnings and errors
- **Development rules** — Document best practices for Obsidian plugin development

### Bug Fixes
- Fix settings page heading using `setHeading()` instead of HTML elements
- Replace inline styles with CSS classes
- Fix Promise handling with `void` operator
- Fix lexical declarations in case blocks
- Remove unused imports and variables
- Use `instanceof` instead of `in` operator for type checking
- Use `Vault.configDir` instead of hardcoded `.obsidian` path

### Technical
- Replace all `error as Error` type assertions with helper functions
- Remove `require()` import, use proper module imports
- Add braces to case blocks with lexical declarations
- Use `window.setTimeout` for popout window compatibility

## Installation

1. Download `hybrid-git-sync-1.0.2.zip`
2. Extract to `.obsidian/plugins/hybrid-git-sync/`
3. Enable the plugin in Obsidian settings

## Changelog

- v1.0.2 — Error handling improvements, code quality fixes
- v1.0.1 — Auto-detect backend, i18n improvements
- v1.0.0 — Initial release

releases.author: walkskyer
releases.description:
# Hybrid Git Sync v1.0.1

## What's Changed

### Improvements
- **Auto-detect backend based on git availability** — Auto mode now checks if git is installed AND if the vault is a git repository, instead of just checking the platform
- **Better backend mode labels** — Options now clearly describe when to use each mode: "Git (git installed)" and "API (mobile or no git)"
- **Auto language detection** — Plugin automatically follows Obsidian's language setting using `getLanguage()` API

### Bug Fixes
- Fix i18n singleton not initialized with app instance
- Fix locale detection to use Obsidian's `getLanguage()` API
- Fix typo in locale detection code
- Add null safety for `syncQueue` in `onunload`

### Code Quality
- Replace deprecated `escape`/`unescape` with `TextEncoder`/`TextDecoder`
- Replace `any` types with `unknown` for logging parameters
- Use `window.setTimeout` instead of `setTimeout`
- Use `Vault.configDir` instead of hardcoded `.obsidian` path
- Remove unused imports and variables
- Add proper TypeScript types to API responses

## Installation

1. Download `hybrid-git-sync-1.0.1.zip`
2. Extract to `.obsidian/plugins/hybrid-git-sync/`
3. Enable the plugin in Obsidian settings

## Changelog

- v1.0.1 — Auto-detect backend, i18n improvements, code quality fixes
- v1.0.0 — Initial release

releases.author: walkskyer
releases.description:
# Hybrid Git Sync v1.0.0

Cross-platform Git sync plugin for Obsidian. Uses native Git on desktop and GitHub API on mobile.

## Features

### Core Sync
- Bidirectional sync (push/pull)
- Incremental sync with parallel transfer (up to 3 concurrent)
- Auto sync (timer/file change trigger)
- Conflict detection and resolution
- .gitignore support (auto-create with localized comments)

### Platform Support
- **Desktop** (Windows/macOS/Linux): Native Git mode
- **Mobile** (Android/iOS): GitHub API mode
- Auto-detect platform and select backend

### User Interface
- Status bar with sync state indicator
- Ribbon icons for quick access
- Commit history view
- Diff view with line-by-line comparison
- Changed files panel
- Conflict resolution modal

### Multi-language Support
- English
- Chinese (中文)
- Auto-detect from system settings

### Utilities
- Settings import/export
- Log viewer and export
- Sync state management
- Network status detection
- Offline queue (sync when network restored)
- Large file detection (>50MB)

## Installation

1. Download `hybrid-git-sync-1.0.0.zip`
2. Extract to `.obsidian/plugins/hybrid-git-sync/`
3. Enable the plugin in Obsidian settings

## Configuration

### API Mode (Mobile)

1. Create a repository on GitHub
2. Generate a Personal Access Token (requires `repo` scope)
3. Configure in plugin settings:
   - Remote URL: `username/repo`
   - API Token: your token
   - Branch: `main`

### Git Mode (Desktop)

1. Ensure Git is installed on your system
2. Initialize Git in vault: `git init`
3. Configure remote: `git remote add origin <url>`
4. The plugin will automatically use system Git

## Commands

| Command | Description |
|---|---|
| Sync now | Sync immediately |
| Pull | Pull only |
| Push | Push only |
| View sync status | View sync status |
| View commit history | View commit history |
| View changes | View changed files |
| Diff current file | Diff current file |
| Restore file from remote | Restore file from remote |
| Switch branch | View/switch branch |
| Toggle auto sync | Toggle auto sync |
| View logs | View logs (copy to clipboard) |
| Export settings | Export settings |
| Import settings | Import settings |
| Clear sync state | Clear sync state |

## Known Limitations

- API mode does not support SSH authentication
- API mode single file limit is 50MB
- Mobile cannot run continuous background sync
- GitLab/Gitea support planned for future release

## Changelog

### v1.0.0
- Initial release
- Core sync functionality (Git + API modes)
- Multi-language support (English/Chinese)
- Conflict detection and resolution
- Commit history and diff views
- Auto sync with file change detection
- Network status awareness
- Settings import/export
- .gitignore auto-creation
